titleStmt.title: SII 11.90: original edition by C. R. Krishnamacharlu, R. S. Panchamukhi, N. Lakshminaraya Rao and H. K. Narasimhaswami
No. 90. (B.K. No. 141 of 1926-27). BAḶAGĀNŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT. ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF VĪRABHADRA.

summary.p: The inscription is much damaged and mutilated. It refers itself to the reign of Traiḷōkyamalladēva (Sōmēśvara I) and mentions a subordinate (name lost) of the king who bore the title ‘the lord of Bēṅgīpura’ and was governing over Purigeṟe Three Hundred and another (name lost); probably Belvoḷa Three Hundred division. It registers a grant of land and refers to Rudraśakti-Jīya, Kētimayya, the god Svayaṁbhu-Trikaṇṭīśvara and Tribhuvanaśakti-Jīya.

The record is dated Śaka 975, Jaya, • • • •, Monday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti. In Jaya, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti occurred not on Monday but on the previous Saturday—December 24, A.D. 1054. For the difference of two days, see Ind. Eph. Vol. I, Part I, page 4, para. 14.
The cyclic year Jaya coincided with Śaka 976 (expired).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
nāśraya śrīpṛthvīvallabhaṁ mahārājādhirāja paramē
mabhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāśrayakuḷatiḷakaṁ cā
ttraiḷōkyamalladēvara vijayarājyamuttarōttarābhivriddhi
caṁdrārkkatāraṁbaraṁ saluttamire . svasti samadhigatapañcama
ḷēsvaraṁ bēṁgīpuravarērveraṁ kumaramārttaṇḍaṁ śrīmatsō
ṣūnūṟuṁpurigeṟe mūnūrumaṁ duṣṭanigraha vi
kathāvinōdadiṁ dāḷuttamire śrīsvayaṁbhutrikaṁ
dēvarige pādapūjeyaṁ muḍi
varṣa 975 neya jaya saṁvatsarada
sōmavāra muttarāyaṇa saṁkrāntiya
dēvara saṁtatiya rudraśaktijīya
maga kētimayyaṁ muriyēśvara
vageṟeya teṁkaṇameyyelu taḷa
13 Adakkaṟuvaṇaṁ gadyā 1
dēvarige śrīsvayaṁbutrikaṁṭīśva
koṇḍu sattu paḍedu tribhuva
śvara dēvargge vidyādānadai
śraya kōloḷ sarvvanamasya
liṅgavināśiyappantu śiḷāsā
baredu koṭṭa dharmmamaṁ pratipāḷi
sirppar . tribhuvanaśaktijīya
nṛpāṇāṁ kāḷēkālē pālanī
bhūyō bhūyō yāca
suṁdharā Two or three more lines are lost. Two lines engraved on the top of the slab are completely
Digital edition of SII 11.90 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
